title: rayjacksonia phyllocephala var. megacephala
intro: Rayjacksonia phyllocephala var. megacephala, also known as the 'Desert Marigold', is a rare and endangered plant species native to the southwestern United States. It is a member of the sunflower family and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, and its health benefits may include reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its availability is highly restricted due to its endangered status.
